Title: [SEC-NS-1] Establish Network Segmentation Boundaries: Virtual Network
Description: Assess NS-1 controls for Virtual Network: address space isolation; subnet design; NSG deny-all defaults; peering security; AVNM admin rules enforcement; hub-spoke topology compliance.

Title: [SEC-NS-1] Establish Network Segmentation Boundaries: Virtual Network NAT
Description: Assess NS-1 controls for Virtual Network NAT: outbound-only connectivity model; no inbound exposure via NAT; public IP prefix management; SNAT exhaustion monitoring.

Title: [SEC-NS-1] Establish Network Segmentation Boundaries: Virtual WAN
Description: Assess NS-1 controls for Virtual WAN: hub segmentation between branches; virtual hub routing policies; secured hub via Firewall Manager integration; spoke isolation.

Title: [SEC-NS-1] Establish Network Segmentation Boundaries: Load Balancer
Description: Assess NS-1 controls for Load Balancer: internal LB for backend pools (no public IP); backend pool subnet isolation; Standard SKU only; health probe security.

Title: [SEC-NS-1] Establish Network Segmentation Boundaries: Traffic Manager
Description: Assess NS-1 controls for Traffic Manager: endpoint health monitoring; HTTPS-only endpoint enforcement; no sensitive data in DNS responses; geographic routing isolation.

Title: [SEC-NS-1] Establish Network Segmentation Boundaries: Peering Service
Description: Assess NS-1 controls for Azure Peering Service: route filtering; BGP community tagging; peering location security; monitoring for unauthorized route changes.

Title: [SEC-NS-1] Establish Network Segmentation Boundaries: NAT Gateway
Description: Assess NS-1 controls for NAT Gateway: association with correct subnets; public IP prefix rotation policy; idle timeout tuning; SNAT port exhaustion alert.

Title: [SEC-NS-2] Secure Cloud Native Services: App Service
Description: Assess NS-2 controls for App Service: VNet integration; private endpoint; inbound access restrictions; HTTPS-only; TLS 1.2 minimum; FTP disabled.

Title: [SEC-NS-2] Secure Cloud Native Services: Cache for Redis
Description: Assess NS-2 controls for Cache for Redis: private endpoint or VNet injection; non-SSL port disabled; TLS 1.2 minimum; no public network access; managed identity for client auth.

Title: [SEC-NS-2] Secure Cloud Native Services: Cognitive Search
Description: Assess NS-2 controls for Cognitive Search: private endpoint; disable public network access; IP firewall rules; managed identity for data source auth.

Title: [SEC-NS-2] Secure Cloud Native Services: Communication Services
Description: Assess NS-2 controls for Communication Services: managed identity for service auth; private connectivity where available; data residency compliance; API access restriction.

Title: [SEC-NS-2] Secure Cloud Native Services: Communications Gateway
Description: Assess NS-2 controls for Communications Gateway: network peering security; SBC connectivity hardening; TLS for SIP signaling; access restrictions to admin plane.

Title: [SEC-NS-2] Secure Cloud Native Services: Container Apps
Description: Assess NS-2 controls for Container Apps: VNet integration; internal-only ingress for internal apps; private DNS zone; environment-level network isolation.

Title: [SEC-NS-2] Secure Cloud Native Services: Data Factory
Description: Assess NS-2 controls for Data Factory: managed private endpoints for all linked services; private endpoint for ADF itself; self-hosted IR in VNet; disable public network access.

Title: [SEC-NS-2] Secure Cloud Native Services: Database Migration Service
Description: Assess NS-2 controls for Database Migration Service: VNet-integrated deployment; private endpoint; outbound-only connectivity to source/target; disable public access.

Title: [SEC-NS-2] Secure Cloud Native Services: Databricks
Description: Assess NS-2 controls for Databricks: VNet injection (no-public-IP mode); private endpoints for workspace and storage; NSG rules per Databricks requirements; secure cluster connectivity.

Title: [SEC-NS-2] Secure Cloud Native Services: Digital Twins
Description: Assess NS-2 controls for Digital Twins: private endpoint; disable public network access; RBAC over API keys; event routing via private Event Grid topic.

Title: [SEC-NS-2] Secure Cloud Native Services: Event Grid
Description: Assess NS-2 controls for Event Grid: private endpoint for topics/domains; disable public network access; managed identity for event delivery; subscriber HTTPS endpoint enforcement.

Title: [SEC-NS-2] Secure Cloud Native Services: Event Hubs
Description: Assess NS-2 controls for Event Hubs: private endpoint; disable public network access; managed identity (no SAS keys); IP firewall; TLS 1.2 minimum.

Title: [SEC-NS-2] Secure Cloud Native Services: File Sync
Description: Assess NS-2 controls for Azure File Sync: storage firewall rules; private endpoint for sync service; TLS for all data transfer; registered server auth via Azure AD.

Title: [SEC-NS-2] Secure Cloud Native Services: Functions
Description: Assess NS-2 controls for Azure Functions: VNet integration; private endpoints for storage triggers; inbound access restrictions; HTTPS-only; FTP disabled; managed identity for bindings.

Title: [SEC-NS-2] Secure Cloud Native Services: HPC Cache
Description: Assess NS-2 controls for HPC Cache: VNet deployment; subnet isolation; no public endpoint; storage target access via private routing; client subnet restrictions.

Title: [SEC-NS-2] Secure Cloud Native Services: Logic Apps
Description: Assess NS-2 controls for Logic Apps (Standard): VNet integration; private endpoint; IP restriction for trigger/callback access; managed identity for connector auth; no hardcoded credentials.

Title: [SEC-NS-2] Secure Cloud Native Services: Machine Learning Service
Description: Assess NS-2 controls for Azure Machine Learning: VNet-isolated workspace; private endpoint; no public workspace access; compute cluster/instance in VNet; no public IP on compute.

Title: [SEC-NS-2] Secure Cloud Native Services: Managed Lustre
Description: Assess NS-2 controls for Azure Managed Lustre: VNet integration; subnet isolation; no public endpoint; NSG rules for client access restriction; storage backend via private routing.

Title: [SEC-NS-2] Secure Cloud Native Services: Notification Hubs
Description: Assess NS-2 controls for Notification Hubs: namespace IP firewall; managed identity for backend API connections; TLS for push notification delivery; no SAS key exposure.

Title: [SEC-NS-2] Secure Cloud Native Services: Remote Rendering
Description: Assess NS-2 controls for Azure Remote Rendering: VNet-based session connectivity; managed identity auth; restrict session access to authorized clients; TLS for streaming.

Title: [SEC-NS-2] Secure Cloud Native Services: Service Bus
Description: Assess NS-2 controls for Service Bus: private endpoint; disable public network access; managed identity (no SAS keys for app auth); IP firewall rules; TLS 1.2 minimum.

Title: [SEC-NS-2] Secure Cloud Native Services: SignalR Service
Description: Assess NS-2 controls for SignalR Service: private endpoint; disable public network access; managed identity for upstream auth; serverless mode connectivity via private link.

Title: [SEC-NS-2] Secure Cloud Native Services: Spring Apps
Description: Assess NS-2 controls for Azure Spring Apps: VNet injection; private endpoint; disable public test endpoint; outbound UDR via Azure Firewall; app-level network isolation.

Title: [SEC-NS-2] Secure Cloud Native Services: Web PubSub
Description: Assess NS-2 controls for Azure Web PubSub: private endpoint; disable public network access; managed identity for upstream webhook auth; TLS for client connections.

Title: [SEC-NS-2] Secure Cloud Native Services: Batch
Description: Assess NS-2 controls for Azure Batch: VNet pool deployment; no public IP on compute nodes; private endpoint for Batch account; managed identity on pools (no storage keys).

Title: [SEC-NS-2] Secure Cloud Native Services: Cognitive Services
Description: Assess NS-2 controls for Cognitive Services (AI Services): private endpoint; disable public network access; managed identity for client auth; VNet service endpoint where private endpoint not supported.

Title: [SEC-NS-2] Secure Cloud Native Services: Content Delivery Network
Description: Assess NS-2 controls for Content Delivery Network: HTTPS-only delivery rules; custom domain TLS; WAF policy via Front Door CDN; origin access control (restrict origin to CDN only).

Title: [SEC-NS-3] Deploy Firewall at Edge of Enterprise Network: Firewall
Description: Assess NS-3 controls for Azure Firewall: Premium SKU with IDPS (Alert+Deny); Threat Intelligence enabled; centralized hub deployment; policy hierarchy (parent + child); diagnostic logging to Log Analytics.

Title: [SEC-NS-3] Deploy Firewall at Edge of Enterprise Network: Firewall Manager
Description: Assess NS-3 controls for Firewall Manager: centralized policy management across hubs; secured virtual hub configuration; DDoS plan association; route intent enforcement for spoke traffic.

Title: [SEC-NS-3] Deploy Firewall at Edge of Enterprise Network: Stack Edge
Description: Assess NS-3 controls for Azure Stack Edge: physical edge local firewall configuration; VPN for cloud connectivity; local user access restriction; firmware update management.

Title: [SEC-NS-4] Deploy Intrusion Detection/Prevention Systems
Description: Enable Azure Firewall Premium IDPS in Alert+Deny mode across all hub firewalls. Signature updates automatic. Alert integration to Sentinel. IDPS alert cadence and false-positive tuning reviewed quarterly.

Title: [SEC-NS-5] Deploy DDoS Protection: DDoS Protection
Description: Assess NS-5 controls for Azure DDoS Protection: Network Protection Standard on all VNets with public IPs; DDoS diagnostic logs to Log Analytics; metric alerts for DDoS attack start/end events.

Title: [SEC-NS-5] Deploy DDoS Protection: Public IP
Description: Assess NS-5 controls for Public IP: DDoS Network Protection Standard association; monitoring for DDoS attack events; unnecessary PIPs removed; Standard SKU only (no Basic PIPs).

Title: [SEC-NS-6] Deploy Web Application Firewall: Application Gateway
Description: Assess NS-6 controls for Application Gateway: WAF v2 Prevention mode; OWASP CRS 3.2; bot protection ruleset; SSL offload TLS 1.2+; WAF diagnostic logging to Sentinel.

Title: [SEC-NS-6] Deploy Web Application Firewall: Front Door
Description: Assess NS-6 controls for Azure Front Door: WAF policy Prevention mode; managed DefaultRuleSet; bot management; DDoS protection; HTTPS redirect enforced; origin access control.

Title: [SEC-NS-6] Deploy Web Application Firewall: Web Application Firewall
Description: Assess NS-6 controls for Azure WAF (standalone policy): Prevention mode; rule group review; custom rules for org-specific threats; exclusion list governed and reviewed.

Title: [SEC-NS-7] Simplify Network Security Configuration: Network Watcher
Description: Assess NS-7 controls for Network Watcher: NSG flow logs v2 on all NSGs → Storage + Traffic Analytics; connection monitor for critical service paths; packet capture policy; effective routes validation.

Title: [SEC-NS-8] Detect and Disable Insecure Services and Protocols
Description: Enumerate and disable TLS 1.0/1.1; SSLv3; unencrypted HTTP; FTP; Telnet; SMBv1 across all Azure services. Azure Policy: enforce TLS minimum version. Defender for Cloud: insecure protocol recommendations reviewed.

Title: [SEC-NS-9] Connect On-Premises or Cloud Network Privately: VPN Gateway
Description: Assess NS-9 controls for VPN Gateway: IKEv2 protocol; BGP authentication (MD5); certificate-based auth; VPN diagnostic logs to Log Analytics; no Basic SKU.

Title: [SEC-NS-9] Connect On-Premises or Cloud Network Privately: Private Link
Description: Assess NS-9 controls for Azure Private Link: service endpoint vs private endpoint gap assessment; DNS zone group configuration; network policies on subnet; approval workflow for cross-tenant connections.

Title: [SEC-NS-9] Connect On-Premises or Cloud Network Privately: Bastion
Description: Assess NS-9 controls for Azure Bastion: Premium SKU; shareable link disabled; session recording enabled; audit logging to Log Analytics; restricted RBAC for Bastion resource.

Title: [SEC-NS-9] Connect On-Premises or Cloud Network Privately: Virtual Desktop
Description: Assess NS-9 controls for Azure Virtual Desktop: private endpoint for host pool; RDP Shortpath enabled; session host VNet isolation; MFA via Conditional Access for WVD access.

Title: [SEC-NS-9] Connect On-Premises or Cloud Network Privately: Nutanix on Azure
Description: Assess NS-9 controls for Nutanix on Azure: dedicated VNet; no public IP on Nutanix nodes; Azure Firewall egress control; Nutanix security hardening for management plane.

Title: [SEC-NS-9] Connect On-Premises or Cloud Network Privately: VMware Solution
Description: Assess NS-9 controls for Azure VMware Solution: ExpressRoute private connectivity; NSX-T microsegmentation for VM workloads; management network isolation; no public access to vCenter/NSX.

Title: [SEC-NS-10] Ensure Domain Name System (DNS) Security: DNS
Description: Assess NS-10 controls for Azure DNS: Private DNS zones for all private endpoints; no public zone for internal resources; conditional forwarder via DNS Private Resolver; Defender for DNS monitoring.

Title: [SEC-IM-1] Use Centralized Identity and Authentication System: Active Directory Domain Services
Description: Assess IM-1 controls for Active Directory Domain Services: AAD Connect health monitoring; hybrid identity sync security; LDAP over TLS (636); domain controller hardening; Tier-0 asset protection.

Title: [SEC-IM-2] Protect Identity and Authentication Systems
Description: Enable Azure AD Identity Protection: user risk and sign-in risk policies (High → block; Medium → MFA). Risky user/sign-in alerts → Sentinel. Smart lockout policy enforced. AAD Connect Health monitoring active.

Title: [SEC-IM-3] Manage Application Identities Securely and Automatically: Bot Service
Description: Assess IM-3 controls for Bot Service: managed identity for bot auth (no stored credentials); Azure AD token validation for Direct Line; HTTPS enforcement on all bot channels.

Title: [SEC-IM-3] Manage Application Identities Securely and Automatically: Universal Print
Description: Assess IM-3 controls for Universal Print: managed identity for print connector authentication; Azure AD group-based print queue access control; no shared credentials for printer registration.

Title: [SEC-IM-4] Authenticate Server and Services: Attestation
Description: Assess IM-4 controls for Azure Attestation: mTLS for attestation requests; policy signing with approved certificates; audit logging; private endpoint for attestation service endpoint.

Title: [SEC-IM-4] Authenticate Server and Services: Trusted Hardware Identity Management
Description: Assess IM-4 controls for Trusted Hardware Identity Management: hardware identity certificate chain validation; managed identity integration; audit logging of certificate issuance and renewal.

Title: [SEC-IM-5] Use Single Sign-On (SSO) for Application Access: API Management
Description: Assess IM-5 controls for API Management: OAuth2/OIDC for API consumer SSO; managed identity for backend service auth; subscription key rotation policy; developer portal Azure AD integration.

Title: [SEC-IM-6] Use Strong Authentication Controls
Description: Enforce MFA via Conditional Access for all users. Block legacy authentication protocols via CA policy. Phishing-resistant MFA (FIDO2/Windows Hello) for privileged users. Number matching + additional context for Authenticator push notifications.

Title: [SEC-IM-7] Restrict Resource Access Based on Conditions: Spatial Anchors
Description: Assess IM-7 controls for Azure Spatial Anchors: Conditional Access for spatial anchor access tokens; RBAC for account management; audit logging of anchor access.

Title: [SEC-IM-8] Restrict the Exposure of Credential and Secrets
Description: All secrets in Key Vault (no plaintext in configs; code; env vars; ADO pipelines). Secret scanning via Defender for DevOps. Service principal secrets: 90-day expiry; automated rotation. Storage shared key auth disabled where feasible.

Title: [SEC-PA-1] Separate and Limit Highly Privileged Users
Description: Inventory all Global Admin; Subscription Owner; and high-privilege role holders. Target: <5 Global Admins. Cloud-only privileged accounts. PIM for all eligible role assignments. Privileged role reduction exercise tracked.

Title: [SEC-PA-2] Avoid Standing Access: Automation
Description: Assess PA-2 controls for Azure Automation: managed identity replacing deprecated RunAs accounts; JIT runbook execution scheduling; private endpoint for Automation account; audit logging of runbook executions.

Title: [SEC-PA-3] Manage Lifecycle of Identities and Entitlements
Description: Entitlement Management: access packages with approval workflow. Automated provisioning/deprovisioning via SCIM. Joiner-Mover-Leaver process defined. Stale account detection (90+ days → auto-disable). Guest account expiry policy (30 days unless renewed).

Title: [SEC-PA-4] Review and Reconcile User Access Regularly
Description: Azure AD Access Reviews: quarterly for privileged roles; semi-annual for group memberships. Reviewer = line manager or resource owner. Auto-deny on no response. Review results feed into deprovisioning workflow.

Title: [SEC-PA-5] Set Up Emergency Access
Description: Two break-glass accounts: Global Admin; cloud-only; no MFA shared with production. Passwords in two physically separate secure locations. Excluded from all CA policies. Sign-in monitored. Tested and validated quarterly.

Title: [SEC-PA-6] Use Privileged Access Workstations: Cloud Shell
Description: Assess PA-6 controls for Cloud Shell: launched only from PAW or Bastion (CA policy: require compliant device); storage account with private endpoint; RBAC on storage account; session audit logging enabled.

Title: [SEC-PA-7] Follow Just Enough Administration (Least Privilege): Lighthouse
Description: Assess PA-7 controls for Azure Lighthouse: managed tenant delegations reviewed for least-privilege roles; no Owner delegation (Contributor max); delegation audit logs reviewed quarterly; customer approval workflow.

Title: [SEC-PA-8] Choose Approval Process for Microsoft Support Access: Customer Lockbox
Description: Assess PA-8 controls for Customer Lockbox: Lockbox enabled at tenant; approver role assigned; approval SLA (12h auto-deny); Lockbox request audit log routed to Sentinel.

Title: [SEC-DP-1] Discover, Classify, and Label Sensitive Data: Information Protection
Description: Assess DP-1 controls for Microsoft Information Protection: sensitivity labels (Public/Internal/Confidential/Restricted); auto-labeling policies; label inheritance for data copied to Azure storage.

Title: [SEC-DP-1] Discover, Classify, and Label Sensitive Data: Purview
Description: Assess DP-1 controls for Microsoft Purview: data catalog scan coverage (Storage; SQL; Synapse; ADLS); classification rules; sensitive data discovery; DLP policy enforcement; insider risk management.

Title: [SEC-DP-1] Discover, Classify, and Label Sensitive Data: Data Share
Description: Assess DP-1 controls for Azure Data Share: data classification before sharing; recipient identity validation; snapshot access review; shared dataset sensitivity labeling.

Title: [SEC-DP-2] Monitor Anomalies and Threats Targeting Sensitive Data: Data Explorer
Description: Assess DP-2 controls for Azure Data Explorer: anomalous query detection via Defender for Databases; row-level security on sensitive tables; query audit logging to Log Analytics; access pattern baselining.

Title: [SEC-DP-2] Monitor Anomalies and Threats Targeting Sensitive Data: OpenAI
Description: Assess DP-2 controls for Azure OpenAI: content filtering enabled (all harm categories); data exfiltration via model responses monitored; input/output logging to Log Analytics; managed identity auth (no API keys in code).

Title: [SEC-DP-2] Monitor Anomalies and Threats Targeting Sensitive Data: Intelligent Recommendations
Description: Assess DP-2 controls for Intelligent Recommendations: data access audit logging; managed identity for model training data access; anomalous recommendation pattern monitoring; no PII in recommendation metadata.

Title: [SEC-DP-3] Encrypt Sensitive Data in Transit: Database for MariaDB
Description: Assess DP-3 controls for Azure Database for MariaDB: TLS 1.2 minimum enforced (ssl_enforce_enabled=ON); disable non-SSL connections; certificate pinning for client connections; TLS version audit via Policy.

Title: [SEC-DP-3] Encrypt Sensitive Data in Transit: Database for MySQL Flexible Server
Description: Assess DP-3 controls for Azure Database for MySQL Flexible Server: TLS 1.2+ required; disable unencrypted connections; HTTPS-only connection strings; minimal TLS version parameter audit.

Title: [SEC-DP-3] Encrypt Sensitive Data in Transit: Database for PostgreSQL Flexible Server
Description: Assess DP-3 controls for PostgreSQL Flexible Server: TLS minimum version enforced; ssl_mode=REQUIRE; disable plaintext connections; certificate validation on client side.

Title: [SEC-DP-3] Encrypt Sensitive Data in Transit: Cosmos DB for PostgreSQL
Description: Assess DP-3 controls for Cosmos DB for PostgreSQL: TLS 1.2 enforcement; SSL required for all client connections; certificate validation; no plaintext PostgreSQL wire protocol connections.

Title: [SEC-DP-3] Encrypt Sensitive Data in Transit: Stream Analytics
Description: Assess DP-3 controls for Stream Analytics: TLS for all input/output connections (Event Hub; Service Bus; SQL; Storage); managed identity for source/sink auth; no plaintext credentials in job configuration.

Title: [SEC-DP-3] Encrypt Sensitive Data in Transit: Media Services
Description: Assess DP-3 controls for Azure Media Services: HTTPS-only streaming delivery; DRM for content protection; managed identity for storage access; TLS for live encoder ingest connections.

Title: [SEC-DP-4] Enable Data at Rest Encryption by Default: Storage
Description: Assess DP-4 controls for Azure Storage: AES-256 encryption at rest; CMK option assessment; disable allow-blob-public-access; infrastructure encryption for Confidential data; immutability for compliance tiers.

Title: [SEC-DP-4] Enable Data at Rest Encryption by Default: SQL
Description: Assess DP-4 controls for Azure SQL Database: TDE with PMK default or CMK review; SQL Auditing enabled → Log Analytics; Always Encrypted for highly sensitive columns; no unencrypted database exports.

Title: [SEC-DP-4] Enable Data at Rest Encryption by Default: SQL IaaS
Description: Assess DP-4 controls for SQL Server on Azure VMs: Azure Disk Encryption on VM disks; TDE on SQL databases; encrypted backups; Key Vault for encryption key management.

Title: [SEC-DP-4] Enable Data at Rest Encryption by Default: Analysis Services
Description: Assess DP-4 controls for Azure Analysis Services: model data encrypted at rest; managed identity for data source connections; row-level security on sensitive models; audit logging.

Title: [SEC-DP-4] Enable Data at Rest Encryption by Default: App Configuration
Description: Assess DP-4 controls for Azure App Configuration: encryption at rest (default AES-256); CMK for Confidential/Restricted configurations; managed identity access; private endpoint.

Title: [SEC-DP-4] Enable Data at Rest Encryption by Default: Center for SAP Solutions
Description: Assess DP-4 controls for Center for SAP Solutions: SAP HANA encryption at rest; managed disk encryption on all SAP VMs; backup encryption via Azure Backup CMK; data classification for SAP landscape.

Title: [SEC-DP-4] Enable Data at Rest Encryption by Default: Data Lake Analytics
Description: Assess DP-4 controls for Azure Data Lake Analytics: ADLS Gen1 encryption at rest (HSM-backed keys); job data encryption; managed identity for compute-to-storage access; no unencrypted job output storage.

Title: [SEC-DP-5] Use Customer-Managed Key When Required: Cosmos DB
Description: Assess DP-5 controls for Azure Cosmos DB: CMK for encryption at rest; managed identity for Key Vault access; private endpoint; disable public network access; role-based access (no master keys in apps).

Title: [SEC-DP-5] Use Customer-Managed Key When Required: Synapse Analytics
Description: Assess DP-5 controls for Azure Synapse Analytics: workspace CMK via managed identity and Key Vault; dedicated SQL pool TDE with CMK; encryption key rotation policy; double encryption for Confidential data.

Title: [SEC-DP-5] Use Customer-Managed Key When Required: Data Manager for Energy
Description: Assess DP-5 controls for Azure Data Manager for Energy: CMK for data-at-rest encryption; customer-owned key management via Key Vault; OSDU encryption compliance; key rotation cadence.

Title: [SEC-DP-5] Use Customer-Managed Key When Required: Managed Instance for Apache Cassandra
Description: Assess DP-5 controls for Managed Instance for Apache Cassandra: CMK for data-at-rest encryption via Key Vault; managed identity; private endpoint; disk encryption on Cassandra nodes.

Title: [SEC-DP-5] Use Customer-Managed Key When Required: NetApp Files
Description: Assess DP-5 controls for Azure NetApp Files: volume encryption via NetApp Encryption; CMK via Azure Key Vault where supported; SMB Kerberos / NFS Kerberos for in-transit security; capacity pool encryption.

Title: [SEC-DP-6] Use a Secure Key Management Process: Key Vault
Description: Assess DP-6 controls for Azure Key Vault: RBAC model (not legacy access policies); soft delete + purge protection; private endpoint; network firewall deny-public; key rotation policy (RSA-2048+; 1-year); all operations audit logged.

Title: [SEC-DP-6] Use a Secure Key Management Process: Key Vault Managed HSM
Description: Assess DP-6 controls for Key Vault Managed HSM: FIPS 140-2 Level 3; built-in RBAC; purge protection; private endpoint; HSM key backup/restore policy tested.

Title: [SEC-DP-6] Use a Secure Key Management Process: Dedicated HSM
Description: Assess DP-6 controls for Azure Dedicated HSM: customer-managed FIPS 140-2 Level 3 device; VNet integration; physical access controls validated; HSM zeroization policy documented; BYOL key management audited.

Title: [SEC-DP-7] Use a Secure Certificate Management Process
Description: All TLS certs managed via Key Vault (App Service; API Management; Application Gateway cert bindings). Certificate expiry monitoring (alert at 30d and 7d). Auto-renewal for integrated certs. No self-signed certs in production.

Title: [SEC-DP-8] Ensure Security of Key and Certificate Repository: Data Box
Description: Assess DP-8 controls for Azure Data Box: physical chain of custody documentation; disk-level encryption during transit (BitLocker); NIST SP 800-88 data wipe on return; BitLocker keys in Key Vault; shipment tracking and tamper-evidence review.

Title: [SEC-AM-1] Track Asset Inventory and Their Risks: Resource Graph
Description: Assess AM-1 controls for Azure Resource Graph: cross-subscription inventory queries; tag compliance reporting; stale resource detection (orphaned disks/NICs/PIPs); security configuration drift queries.

Title: [SEC-AM-1] Track Asset Inventory and Their Risks: Migrate
Description: Assess AM-1 controls for Azure Migrate: discovered asset inventory accuracy; assessment data sensitivity; migration readiness security review; dependency analysis data access restriction.

Title: [SEC-AM-2] Use Only Approved Services: Policy
Description: Assess AM-2 controls for Azure Policy (resource allowlist): allowed resource types initiative at management group; deny unapproved resource types; allowed locations enforcement; Marketplace restriction policy.

Title: [SEC-AM-3] Ensure Security of Asset Lifecycle Management: Resource Mover
Description: Assess AM-3 controls for Azure Resource Mover: RBAC for move operations; security control validation post-move (NSG; private endpoint; tags); move collection audit logging; rollback capability tested.

Title: [SEC-AM-3] Ensure Security of Asset Lifecycle Management: DevTest Labs
Description: Assess AM-3 controls for DevTest Labs: approved VM image policy (marketplace images only); auto-shutdown enforced; cost limit enforcement; lab RBAC (no Owner for lab users); artifact source security.

Title: [SEC-AM-4] Limit Access to Asset Management: Resource Manager
Description: Assess AM-4 controls for Azure Resource Manager: management plane RBAC least privilege; CanNotDelete locks on production resources; ARM template deployment audit; no broad Contributor at tenant root.

Title: [SEC-AM-5] Use Only Approved Applications in Virtual Machine
Description: Defender for Cloud Adaptive Application Controls allowlist on production VMs. Software inventory via AMA. Detect and alert on unapproved software. Windows: AppLocker or WDAC policy. Linux: file integrity monitoring via MDE.

Title: [SEC-LT-1] Enable Threat Detection Capabilities: Defender for Cloud
Parent Title: [SEC-LT] Logging and Threat Detection — MCSB v2
Description: Assess LT-1 controls for Microsoft Defender for Cloud: all Defender plans enabled (Servers P2; Storage; SQL; Containers; AppService; KeyVault; DNS; ARM; DevOps); MDE auto-provisioning for all VMs; alert routing to Sentinel.

Title: [SEC-LT-2] Enable Threat Detection for Identity and Access Management
Parent Title: [SEC-LT] Logging and Threat Detection — MCSB v2
Description: Azure AD Identity Protection sign-in/user risk policies active. Entra ID Audit + Sign-in logs → Sentinel. Sentinel analytics rules: impossible travel; password spray; legacy auth; MFA fatigue. UEBA enabled.

Title: [SEC-LT-3] Enable Logging for Security Investigation
Parent Title: [SEC-LT] Logging and Threat Detection — MCSB v2
Description: Azure Policy DeployIfNotExists: deploy diagnostic settings on all resource types → Log Analytics. Azure Activity Log forwarded. Resource log retention: 90 days interactive + 1 year archive. Remediation tasks executed for non-compliant resources.

Title: [SEC-LT-4] Enable Network Logging for Security Investigation
Parent Title: [SEC-LT] Logging and Threat Detection — MCSB v2
Description: NSG Flow Logs v2 on all NSGs → Storage + Traffic Analytics enabled. Azure Firewall diagnostic logs → Log Analytics. Application Gateway access/WAF logs forwarded. DNS query logs via Defender for DNS.

Title: [SEC-LT-5] Centralize Security Log Management and Analysis: Sentinel
Parent Title: [SEC-LT] Logging and Threat Detection — MCSB v2
Description: Assess LT-5 controls for Microsoft Sentinel: SIEM workspace configuration; data connector coverage (all Azure log sources); analytics rules mapped to MCSB v2 domains; RBAC for SOC analysts; incident assignment workflow.

Title: [SEC-LT-5] Centralize Security Log Management and Analysis: Monitor
Parent Title: [SEC-LT] Logging and Threat Detection — MCSB v2
Description: Assess LT-5 controls for Azure Monitor: diagnostic settings deployment via Azure Policy (DeployIfNotExists); Log Analytics workspace consolidation; alert rules for critical security events; data collection rules (DCRs) for AMA.

Title: [SEC-LT-6] Configure Log Storage Retention
Parent Title: [SEC-LT] Logging and Threat Detection — MCSB v2
Description: Log Analytics workspace: 90 days interactive retention. Auxiliary Logs tier for 1-year retention. Archive tier for 2-year compliance retention. Sentinel retention aligned. Storage Account WORM immutability for archived security logs.

Title: [SEC-LT-7] Use Approved Time Synchronization Sources
Parent Title: [SEC-LT] Logging and Threat Detection — MCSB v2
Description: Azure VMs: Windows Time synced to Azure NTP (168.63.129.16). All VMs verified for consistent time (time skew >1min alert). Arc-enabled servers: NTP sync verification. Consistent timestamps required for Sentinel correlation accuracy.

Title: [SEC-IR-1] Establish an Incident Response Plan and Handling
Description: IR plan documented covering PICERL phases. Runbooks for: ransomware; account compromise; data exfiltration. Sentinel playbooks (Logic Apps) for automated containment. IR team contacts and escalation path documented. Annual IR plan review scheduled.

Title: [SEC-IR-2] Preparation: Setup Incident Notification
Description: Security contact (email + phone) in Defender for Cloud subscription settings. Alert notification for all severity levels. Action Groups: email + SMS + Teams webhook for High severity. Escalation matrix documented and tested.

Title: [SEC-IR-3] Detection and Analysis: Create Incidents Based on High Quality Alerts
Description: Sentinel analytics rules reviewed and tuned for all MCSB v2 domains. Alert suppression for known false positives (with approval). Alert quality metric: false positive rate <20%. UEBA entity insights used in triage. MDTI threat intelligence integrated.

Title: [SEC-IR-4] Detection and Analysis: Investigate an Incident
Description: Sentinel investigation graph available and SOC trained. Log queries documented for: lateral movement; data exfiltration; privilege escalation. MDE live response capability tested. Evidence collection SOP documented.

Title: [SEC-PV-1] Run Automated Vulnerability Scans: Defender for Cloud
Parent Title: [SEC-PV] Posture and Vulnerability Management — MCSB v2
Description: Assess PV-1 controls for Defender for Cloud (Secure Score / posture angle): Defender for Servers P2 VA (Qualys/MDE-based); Secure Score ≥70% target; Critical CVE patch SLA 48h; High CVE 7-day SLA; container image scanning.

Title: [SEC-PV-2] Run Automated OS Patch Management
Parent Title: [SEC-PV] Posture and Vulnerability Management — MCSB v2
Description: Azure Update Manager: patch assessment and deployment schedules for all VMs (Windows + Linux). Maintenance windows per environment. Critical patches within 48h. Monthly patch compliance report. Arc-enabled servers covered.

Title: [SEC-PV-3] Establish Secure Configurations for Compute Resources
Parent Title: [SEC-PV] Posture and Vulnerability Management — MCSB v2
Description: CIS Benchmark Level 1 applied as baseline for all Windows and Linux VMs. Hardened golden images. Azure Machine Configuration (Guest Policy) for continuous OS configuration assessment. Container base images: distroless or hardened.

Title: [SEC-PV-4] Audit and Enforce Secure Configurations for Compute Resources
Parent Title: [SEC-PV] Posture and Vulnerability Management — MCSB v2
Description: Azure Policy Machine Configuration: enforce CIS benchmarks on VMs. Remediation tasks automated for non-compliant. ≥90% compliant VM target. OPA Gatekeeper on AKS (deny privileged pods; require resource limits).

Title: [SEC-PV-5] Perform Vulnerability Assessments: Advisor
Parent Title: [SEC-PV] Posture and Vulnerability Management — MCSB v2
Description: Assess PV-5 controls for Azure Advisor: security recommendations review and remediation tracking (ADO integration); recommendation suppression governance; integration with Defender for Cloud security recommendation feed.

Title: [SEC-PV-6] Rapidly and Automatically Remediate Vulnerabilities
Parent Title: [SEC-PV] Posture and Vulnerability Management — MCSB v2
Description: Defender for Cloud one-click remediation and automated remediation tasks. Azure Policy remediation tasks scheduled. Vulnerability → ADO work item automation for Critical/High CVEs. Container image rebuild automation on base image CVE detection.

Title: [SEC-PV-7] Conduct Regular Red Team Operations
Parent Title: [SEC-PV] Posture and Vulnerability Management — MCSB v2
Description: Annual red team exercise: external attack surface; identity attack paths; lateral movement. Purple team follow-up. Defender for Cloud attack path analysis and exposure management reviewed. Attack surface reduction metrics tracked.

Title: [SEC-ES-1] Use Endpoint Detection and Response (EDR): Virtual Machines (Linux)
Description: Assess ES-1 controls for Virtual Machines (Linux): MDE for Linux deployed via Defender for Cloud auto-provisioning; EDR in Block mode; MDE telemetry → Sentinel; real-time protection active; onboarding compliance ≥99%.

Title: [SEC-ES-1] Use Endpoint Detection and Response (EDR): Virtual Machines (Windows)
Description: Assess ES-1 controls for Virtual Machines (Windows): MDE deployed via Defender for Cloud auto-provisioning; EDR in Block mode; MDE connected to Sentinel; Defender Antivirus real-time protection; tamper protection enabled; onboarding compliance ≥99%.

Title: [SEC-ES-1] Use Endpoint Detection and Response (EDR): Virtual Machine Scale Sets
Description: Assess ES-1 controls for Virtual Machine Scale Sets: MDE extension in VMSS model definition; auto-provisioning ensures new instances onboarded; uniform EDR telemetry from all scale set instances; VMSS security baseline.

Title: [SEC-ES-1] Use Endpoint Detection and Response (EDR): Arc-enabled Servers
Description: Assess ES-1 controls for Arc-enabled Servers: MDE onboarding via Arc policy extension; EDR telemetry to same Sentinel workspace; patch management via Azure Update Manager; hybrid endpoint inventory in Defender for Cloud.

Title: [SEC-ES-1] Use Endpoint Detection and Response (EDR): Arc-enabled Kubernetes
Description: Assess ES-1 controls for Arc-enabled Kubernetes: Defender for Containers on Arc-managed cluster; runtime threat detection; Kubernetes audit log → Sentinel; cluster security posture in Defender for Cloud.

Title: [SEC-ES-2] Use Modern Anti-Malware Software
Description: Microsoft Defender Antivirus real-time protection on all Windows VMs (cloud-delivered protection on). Defender for Endpoint antimalware on Linux VMs. Scheduled scan policy. Antimalware exclusions documented; reviewed; and minimized. Coverage compliance report reviewed weekly.

Title: [SEC-ES-3] Ensure Anti-Malware Software and Signatures Updated: Container Registry
Description: Assess ES-3 controls for Azure Container Registry: Defender for Containers registry scanning; no Critical CVEs in production images; Notary v2 content trust (signed images only); pull-through cache security policy.

Title: [SEC-ES-3] Ensure Anti-Malware Software and Signatures Updated: Container Instances
Description: Assess ES-3 controls for Container Instances: images from trusted ACR only (no Docker Hub); vulnerability scan pre-deployment; no privileged containers; runtime security coverage via Defender for Containers.

Title: [SEC-ES-3] Ensure Anti-Malware Software and Signatures Updated: Kubernetes Service (AKS)
Description: Assess ES-3 controls for Azure Kubernetes Service: Defender for Containers runtime protection; image scanning at registry and runtime; OPA Gatekeeper (deny latest tag; no privileged pods; require resource limits); AppArmor/seccomp profiles.

Title: [SEC-ES-3] Ensure Anti-Malware Software and Signatures Updated: Kubernetes Service on Azure Stack HCI
Description: Assess ES-3 controls for AKS on Azure Stack HCI: Defender for Containers on HCI cluster; container image scanning; Arc-managed security policy enforcement; HCI node update and patch management.

Title: [SEC-ES-3] Ensure Anti-Malware Software and Signatures Updated: Red Hat OpenShift (ARO)
Description: Assess ES-3 controls for Azure Red Hat OpenShift: Defender for Containers integration; container image registry scanning; OCP Security Context Constraints (SCCs) enforced; MDE for node-level EDR on ARO compute.

Title: [SEC-ES-3] Ensure Anti-Malware Software and Signatures Updated: Defender for IoT
Description: Assess ES-3 controls for Microsoft Defender for IoT: OT sensor deployment on OT/ICS networks; device inventory completeness; anomaly detection for ICS/SCADA protocols (Modbus; DNP3; EtherNet/IP); alert integration to Sentinel.

Title: [SEC-ES-3] Ensure Anti-Malware Software and Signatures Updated: IoT Hub
Description: Assess ES-3 controls for Azure IoT Hub: device attestation (TPM/X.509); DPS enrollment group security; per-device credentials (no shared symmetric keys); Defender for IoT integration for device anomaly detection.

Title: [SEC-ES-3] Ensure Anti-Malware Software and Signatures Updated: IoT Central
Description: Assess ES-3 controls for Azure IoT Central: device credential security (X.509 preferred over SAS); role-based access for operators vs administrators; data export encryption; Defender for IoT integration.

Title: [SEC-ES-3] Ensure Anti-Malware Software and Signatures Updated: Sphere
Description: Assess ES-3 controls for Azure Sphere: OS security update enforcement (automatic updates mandatory); certificate-based device auth; application allow-listing (signed apps only); Sphere Security Service fleet monitoring.

Title: [SEC-BR-1] Ensure Regular Automated Backups: Backup
Description: Assess BR-1 controls for Azure Backup: policy coverage for all production VMs; SQL; Blob Storage; Azure Files; Key Vault; geo-redundant Recovery Services vault; RPO/RTO defined per workload tier; immutable vault enabled.

Title: [SEC-BR-2] Protect Backup and Recovery Data
Description: Backup vault: immutable vault enabled; soft delete (14-day); CMK for vault encryption. Separate RBAC for Backup Operator role (no production Owner access). Cross-subscription restore restrictions enforced. Backup data access reviewed quarterly.

Title: [SEC-BR-3] Monitor Backups
Description: Azure Backup alerts for failed backup jobs → Action Group notification. Backup report via Azure Monitor Workbook. Alert triggers: job failure; backup not run in >25h; policy non-compliance. Weekly backup health review scheduled.

Title: [SEC-BR-4] Regularly Test Backup: Site Recovery
Description: Assess BR-4 controls for Azure Site Recovery: replication health monitoring; quarterly failover test execution; RTO measurement vs target; failback validation in isolated environment; recovery plan reviewed semi-annually.

Title: [SEC-DS-1] Conduct Threat Modeling
Description: Threat modeling using STRIDE methodology for all new infra and application designs. Microsoft Threat Modeling Tool or OWASP Threat Dragon. Design-phase review required (not post-deployment). Threat model artifacts stored in repo. Annual review cadence for existing systems.

Title: [SEC-DS-2] Ensure Software Supply Chain Security
Description: Dependency scanning (Dependabot or Defender for DevOps) in all pipelines. SCA as CI gate. SBOM generation for all container builds. Trusted registry only (ACR with Notary v2 content trust). Package allowlist for npm/PyPI/NuGet.

Title: [SEC-DS-3] Secure DevOps Infrastructure
Description: ADO: MFA enforced; no PAT in code (secret scanning active); PAT expiry ≤90 days. Pipeline service connections use Workload Identity Federation (no stored secrets). ADO audit log → Sentinel. Private build agents in VNet.

Title: [SEC-DS-4] Integrate Static Application Security Testing
Description: SAST in CI pipeline: CodeQL for application code; Checkov for IaC (Terraform/Bicep/ARM). Block merge on Critical SAST findings. Secret detection via pre-commit hooks and pipeline scanning. Defender for DevOps IaC scanning integrated.

Title: [SEC-DS-5] Integrate Dynamic Application Security Testing
Description: DAST scan on staging before production deploy (OWASP ZAP or equivalent). API security testing (OWASP API Top 10). Block production release on Critical DAST findings. DAST scan results tracked in ADO. Cadence: every release for internet-facing services.

Title: [SEC-DS-6] Enforce Security of Workload Throughout DevOps Lifecycle
Description: Security gates in CI/CD: SAST; DAST; dependency scan; container image scan; IaC scan — all mandatory before production deploy. Branch protection: PR review + pipeline pass required. Production deploy approval gate. Drift detection weekly.

Title: [SEC-GS-1] Align Organization Roles, Responsibilities, and Accountabilities: Cost Management
Description: Assess GS-1 controls for Azure Cost Management: RBAC for cost visibility; budget alerts per subscription/department; cost anomaly detection; departmental cost chargeback RACI aligned with security domain owners.

Title: [SEC-GS-2] Define and Implement Enterprise Segmentation Strategy
Description: Management group hierarchy per business unit. Separate subscriptions for prod/dev/sandbox. Separation of duties: DevOps cannot approve own PRs or deploy to prod without second approval. Segmentation policy documented and reviewed annually.

Title: [SEC-GS-3] Define and Implement Data Protection Strategy
Description: Data classification policy: 4 tiers (Public; Internal; Confidential; Restricted). Encryption requirements per tier. Data residency requirements documented. Key management policy (CMK for Confidential/Restricted). DLP policy coverage goal. Reviewed annually.

Title: [SEC-GS-4] Define and Implement Network Security Strategy
Description: Network security strategy: zero-trust principles; private-by-default PaaS; hub-spoke topology; internet egress only via Azure Firewall Premium. AVNM security admin rules governance. Strategy reviewed annually.

Title: [SEC-GS-5] Define and Implement Security Posture Management Strategy: Policy
Description: Assess GS-5 controls for Azure Policy (posture management): MCSB v2 initiative assigned at management group; Regulatory Compliance dashboard reviewed monthly; Secure Score target ≥70%; risk acceptance process documented.

Title: [SEC-GS-6] Define and Implement Identity and Privileged Access Strategy
Description: Identity strategy: Entra ID as sole IdP; PIM for all privileged roles; zero standing access; phishing-resistant MFA (FIDO2) for all admins. Passwordless adoption roadmap. Identity governance maturity model. Strategy reviewed annually.

Title: [SEC-GS-7] Define and Implement Logging, Threat Detection and IR Strategy
Description: Logging strategy: centralized Sentinel workspace; log retention policy; threat detection coverage map per MCSB v2 domain. IR strategy: Sentinel as SOAR; playbook library; MTTD and MTTR metrics tracked. Reviewed annually.

Title: [SEC-GS-8] Define and Implement Backup and Recovery Strategy: Managed Applications
Description: Assess GS-8 controls for Azure Managed Applications: publisher identity verification; managed resource group lock (deny customer modification); application definition security review; managed identity for automation within managed app.

Title: [SEC-GS-9] Define and Implement Endpoint Security Strategy
Description: Endpoint strategy: MDE on all VMs; EDR telemetry to Sentinel; patch SLAs (Critical 48h; High 7d); Defender for Containers for container workloads. Endpoint lifecycle: golden image; hardened baseline; decommission process. Reviewed annually.

Title: [SEC-GS-10] Define and Implement DevOps Security Strategy
Description: DevSecOps strategy: SAST/DAST in CI; IaC scanning; supply chain (SBOM; signed images); security champion program. Security gates: no Critical findings shipped. SLSA Level 2 target. Reviewed annually.
